content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To      :   Dan, Bob S., Ken F., Natalie, Mike                    Date  :   May 5, 1976

                                                                                746-258

From  :   FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
          Ted Prim

Subject:  PROPOSED REGULATION CONCERNING THE MEANING OF
          "PERSON OR COMBINATION OF PERSONS" WITHIN THE MEANING
          OF §82013.

          This proposed regulation was motivated by
Opinion 75-205, Lumsdon, in which the Commission
was asked whether a person, of the corporation in which
that person is the majority shareholder, and the business of
which he is president are a person or combination of persons
which constitute a committee within the meaning of §82013.
I believe strongly that we should find a standard which does
not require a fact intensive analysis to resolve each question in this area. We do not have the staff to make such
investigations and, in addition, such an approach provides
little in the way of predictability for the public. Accordingly, subsection (a) of the proposed regs suggest several
per se rules which may resolve a number of problems in this
area. However, this reg only represents a first shot so I am
actively soliciting your suggestions on how to approach
this problem.

          We are required to notice a reg by May 12 so please
have your comments to me by Monday, May 10.

          Thank you.



TP:pvp

DRAFT 1A                    TP:pvp
May 5, 1976


18213.    Persons or Combination of Persons (82013)

A.    A person as used in Government Code §82013 includes

      (1)  A corporation and its majority shareholder.  A

major shareholder is defined as any person or

entity which owns more than a 50% interest in the

voting stock of the corporation.

      (2)  A partnership and any partner who owns more than

a 50% interest in the profits or capital stock of the

partnership exclusive of any interest held by a limited

partner.

B.    A combination of persons as used in Government Code

§82013 is two or more persons or entities who enter into

an agreement or initial understanding, tacit or otherwise,

that they will coordinate their efforts or work together

to receive contributions or make expenditures for

the common purpose of influencing or attempting

to influence the action of the voters for or against

the nomination or election of one or more candidates

or the qualification or passage of a measure.

sections.question_synthetic: How should the FPPC define 'person or combination of persons' under Government Code §82013 to establish clear per se rules rather than requiring fact-intensive analysis for each case?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC proposes regulatory definitions establishing per se rules: a 'person' includes a corporation and its majority shareholder (>50% voting stock owner) or a partnership and partners owning >50% of profits/capital; a 'combination of persons' means two or more entities with an agreement or understanding to coordinate efforts for common political purposes.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Internal memorandum, not a formal advice letter response to a requester. Includes draft regulatory language (subsections A and B) defining key terms. No traditional QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections, but contains substantive policy proposal. Document appears to be a working draft soliciting internal feedback before May 12 regulation notice deadline.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: How should the FPPC define 'person or combination of persons' under Government Code §82013 to establish clear per se rules rather than requiring fact-intensive analysis for each case?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC proposes regulatory definitions establishing per se rules: a 'person' includes a corporation and its majority shareholder (>50% voting stock owner) or a partnership and partners owning >50% of profits/capital; a 'combination of persons' means two or more entities with an agreement or understanding to coordinate efforts for common political purposes.

This proposed regulation was motivated by Opinion 75-205, Lumsdon, in which the Commission was asked whether a person, of the corporation in which that person is the majority shareholder, and the business of which he is president are a person or combination of persons which constitute a committee within the meaning of §82013. I believe strongly that we should find a standard which does not require a fact intensive analysis to resolve each question in this area. We do not have the staff to make such investigations and, in addition, such an approach provides little in the way of predictability for the public. Accordingly, subsection (a) of the proposed regs suggest several per se rules which may resolve a number of problems in this area. A person as used in Government Code §82013 includes (1) A corporation and its majority shareholder. A major shareholder is defined as any person or entity which owns more than a 50% interest in the voting stock of the corporation. embedding.summary: Internal FPPC memorandum dated May 5, 1976, from Ted Prim proposing draft regulations to define 'person or combination of persons' under §82013, motivated by Opinion Response 75-205 (Lumsden), with suggested per se rules to avoid fact-intensive case-by-case analysis and requesting staff comments by May 10.
